name: Martin 2012 Tadalafil Becker Muscular Dystrophy Muscle Ischemia Extract
population_or_context: Men with Becker muscular dystrophy and dystrophin/nNOS-related exercise muscle ischemia.
sample_size: Men with Becker muscular dystrophy; abstract notes restoration in nine of ten patients in linked figure text, but extraction here relies on abstract-level study description.
methods: Randomized placebo-controlled crossover test of tadalafil on exercise-induced attenuation of reflex vasoconstriction and muscle oxygenation during exercise.
population_profile: Men with Becker muscular dystrophy; disease-specific male evidence with low directness for healthy users.
tadalafil_exposure: Single tadalafil dose; abstract does not specify dose in the extracted text.
indication_context: Experimental muscular dystrophy pathophysiology and possible treatment target.
safety_findings: Abstract reports exercise-specific blood-flow restoration and says effects did not appear to involve systemic blood pressure in linked full text snippets; detailed adverse events not extracted.
directness_to_target_population: Low for healthy men because the mechanism depends on dystrophin/nNOS mislocalization in Becker muscular dystrophy.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Rare disease mechanism, small sample, physiologic muscle oxygenation endpoint, no healthy-user performance endpoint, and abstract-level extraction.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil evidence, but disease-specific nNOS/dystrophin biology limits transfer to healthy muscle performance.
